title: The Communication Gap
stat: Only 21% of personnel read emails within 4 days; average read time has dropped to 9 seconds
implication: Critical updates and operational information are not reaching the people who need them.
use_it: Opens Evertel and Protect conversations: 'If email is your urgent channel, your urgent channel has a 21% open rate.'

title: Rising Hazard Volatility
stat: Climate-driven disasters and civil unrest are both intensifying — more frequent, overlapping incidents
implication: Agencies face simultaneous fire, flood, hazmat, and civil threats with tools designed for one incident at a time.
use_it: Sets up the multi-hazard platform argument vs. point solutions bought for the last disaster.

title: Slow Operational Workflows
stat: Traditional notification workflows take 45–60 minutes to launch
implication: Too slow for fast-moving fires, civil unrest, hazmat, and other time-critical incidents.
use_it: The 'reflex time' conversation: ask them to walk through their actual incident-to-alert timeline, minute by minute.

title: Infrastructure Fragility
stat: In Maui, power loss took cellular offline; in LA, overwhelmed networks became unusable
implication: Digital-only alerting fails precisely when it matters most.
use_it: The redundancy argument for acoustics — and the SAFECOM compliance angle for grant narratives.

title: Fragmented Operations & Redundancy Gaps
stat: Police, Fire, and EM operate on different systems with dangerous silos
implication: Most agencies lack sufficient redundancy when primary systems fail, and no shared operating picture exists across agencies.
use_it: The common-operating-picture demo: one map, every agency, zone-level handoff.
